document.title: TkVillage System API Reference
document.purpose: Provide a full user-facing API reference for the current TkVillage kernel.
author: Codex

orientation.primary-audience: LLM coding agents that need exact callable contracts while editing TkVillage applications.
orientation.secondary-audience: Human Python programmers using the TkVillage package.
notes: This reference documents the current implementation, not planned future behavior.
Functions are procedural; no public classes are required.
The runtime assumes one active TkVillage application per Python process.

rt.description: Runtime module re-exported as tkvillage.rt. Use this for inspection and advanced integration.
rt.caution: Do not reassign runtime containers. reset_app() preserves their identities.

create_app.signature: create_app(name="tkvillage", project_dir_name=".tkvillage", project_root=None, tick_interval_ms=50, test_mode=False, create_root=True)
create_app.description: Reset and initialize the single TkVillage runtime.

parameters.name: Application/runtime name stored in rt.g["name"].
parameters.project_root: Directory under which project_dir_name is resolved. Defaults to current working directory.
parameters.test_mode: When true, tick_once() re-raises exceptions after logging them.
parameters.create_root: When true, create and withdraw the Tk root immediately.

side-effects: Creates hidden Tk root when create_root is true.
Installs Ctrl+Shift+D debug shortcut when a root exists.
raises: Tkinter/Tcl exceptions when create_root is true and Tk is unavailable or broken.

reset_app.description: Reset all runtime state to defaults while preserving dict/list identities.
side-effects: Clears runtime registries, logs, debug state, and id counters.

run.description: Ensure the hidden root exists, start the tick loop, and enter tkinter mainloop.

shutdown.description: Stop ticking, destroy live windows, destroy root, and mark runtime not running.

parameters.name: Configuration key string.
parameters.type: One of string, int, float, bool, path, choice.

raises: ValueError for unknown config type.
ValueError when type is choice and choices is empty.
side-effects: Initializes rt.config_values[name] to default if absent.

load_config.description: Read config.json from the project directory and apply declared type coercions.
raises: File and JSON exceptions if config.json exists but cannot be read or parsed.
ValueError or coercion exceptions for invalid persisted values.

save_config.description: Write rt.config_values to config.json in the project directory.
side-effects: Writes indented JSON with sorted keys.

get_config.description: Return the current config value for name.
raises: KeyError if the key is absent.

set_config.description: Set and optionally persist a config value.

parameters.name: Configuration key string.
parameters.persist: When true, call save_config() after setting.

side-effects: May auto-declare undeclared keys using inferred type.
Writes config.json when persist is true.
raises: ValueError or coercion exceptions for invalid values.

list_config.description: Return declared config rows with current values.
list_config.returns: List of config declaration records plus a value field.

register_window_kind.signature: register_window_kind(window_kind, title=None, multiplicity="singleton", create=None, make_initial_state=None, reduce_event=None, project=None, on_show=None, on_close=None, on_destroy=None, on_tick=None, debug_label=None)
register_window_kind.description: Register a summonable Toplevel window kind.

parameters.title: Window title; defaults to window_kind.
parameters.multiplicity: singleton, per-key, or free-instance. embedded and modal are recognized but not implemented.
parameters.create: Function create(rt, record) that builds widgets.
parameters.make_initial_state: Function make_initial_state(rt, key=None, payload=None) returning state dict.
parameters.reduce_event: Function reduce_event(rt, state, event) returning new_state, effects.
parameters.project: Function project(rt, record) updating widgets from state.
parameters.on_close: Optional hook on_close(rt, record); return False to cancel close.
parameters.on_tick: Optional hook on_tick(rt, record) returning effects or None.

raises: ValueError for unknown multiplicity.
NotImplementedError for embedded or modal multiplicity.

summon_window.description: Create or raise a window according to its registered multiplicity policy.

parameters.key: Required for per-key windows; ignored for singleton; used in instance identity.
parameters.payload: Optional payload passed to make_initial_state and stored in record.

side-effects: Calls create(), project(), raise_window(), and on_show hook as applicable.
raises: KeyError if window kind is not registered.
ValueError if per-key window is summoned without key.
Tkinter exceptions if Tk cannot create a Toplevel.

raise_window.description: Best-effort deiconify, lift, and focus a live window.
raises: KeyError if window_id is absent.

close_window.description: Request close for a window, honoring on_close cancellation.
close_window.returns: True when destroyed; False when on_close returns False.

destroy_window.description: Destroy and unregister a live window if it exists.
notes: If the window_id is absent, this function returns without error.

raises: KeyError if window_id is absent.

post_event.description: Append an event to a target inbox and log it.
raises: KeyError if target_id is absent.

post_event_to_instance.description: Post an event to the live window associated with an instance key.
raises: KeyError if instance_key is absent.

post_event_to_kind.description: Post an event to a singleton window kind.

broadcast_event.description: Post a shallow copy of event to every current target.

parameters.handler: Function handler(rt, target, event) returning effects or None.

send_message.description: Wrap a payload in a MESSAGE envelope, log it, and post it to a target.
side-effects: Appends MESSAGE envelope to target inbox through post_event().
raises: KeyError if target_id is absent.

send_message_to_window.description: Send a MESSAGE envelope to a live window target.

send_message_to_instance.description: Send a MESSAGE envelope to a live window found by instance key.
raises: KeyError if instance_key is absent.

send_message_to_kind.description: Send a MESSAGE envelope to a singleton window kind.

broadcast_message.description: Send a shallow copy of message to every current target.

route_effect.description: Route one reducer/service effect through the runtime.

effect-types.POST_EVENT: Requires target_id and event; posts event.
effect-types.SEND_MESSAGE: Requires target_id and message; sends message.
effect-types.SUMMON_WINDOW: Requires window_kind; optional key and payload.
effect-types.REQUEST_PROJECT: Marks a window for projection using effect.window_id or source_id.

side-effects: Unknown effects are logged unless rt.g["strict_effects"] is true.
raises: ValueError for unknown effects when strict_effects is true.
Effect-specific KeyError or tkinter exceptions.

route_effects.description: Route each effect in effects. None or empty effects are allowed.

start_tick_loop.description: Mark runtime running and schedule the next tick.

side-effects: Cancels pending root.after callback when possible.

raises: Re-raises tick exceptions when rt.g["test_mode"] is true.

run_ticks.description: Run tick_once() repeatedly, optionally flushing Tk update calls after each tick.

parameters.update_tk: When true and root exists, call update_idletasks() and update().

toggle_debug.description: Toggle debug mode. When enabling, summon the runtime dashboard.
toggle_debug.returns: Debug dashboard record when enabling; false when disabling.

get_debug_snapshot.description: Build a JSON-friendly runtime debug snapshot.

parameters.selected_window_id: Optional live window id whose state should be included as selected_window_state.

copy_debug_snapshot.description: Create a debug snapshot as indented JSON text and copy it to the Tk clipboard when root exists.

tkvillage.testing.run_until.description: Testing helper not re-exported from top-level tkvillage. Advances ticks until predicate is true, timeout expires, or max_ticks is reached.

tkvillage.cli.main.description: Console entrypoint for minimal TkVillage CLI.

commands.run [entrypoint]: Run optional Python entrypoint then enter TkVillage mainloop.
commands.config-set <name> <value>: Set and persist one config value.

create.responsibility: Build widgets in record["toplevel"] and store important handles in record["widgets"].

on_close.returns: False to cancel close; any other value allows close.

notes: Unknown effects are logged unless strict_effects is true.

current-limitations: embedded and modal window policies are not implemented.
worker_records is reserved but no worker API is currently public.
The CLI creates a runtime before running an entrypoint; entrypoints that call create_app() will reset that runtime.
The current top-level API does not re-export testing.run_until.
